_comment: Pyright / Pylance configuration scoped to the apsis-py crate.
Why a separate file (and not just [tool.pyright] in pyproject.toml):
Pyright looks for configuration in the directory it is invoked from
and parents thereof. When the IDE is rooted at the workspace top
(the gravity-sim repo root), the per-crate pyproject.toml is
outside that search path and its [tool.pyright] block is silently
ignored. A pyrightconfig.json placed alongside the crate sources
is discovered by pyright through its own walk and applied without
requiring the IDE to be re-rooted into the crate.
The settings here mirror [tool.pyright] in pyproject.toml so
IDE invocations agree on the same checking discipline.
extraPaths: tells pyright to treat python/ as a source root so
imports of `apsis` resolve through the local package even when
the extension has not yet been installed into the active venv.
reportMissingImports: kept on as a warning, not an error; this
lets a contributor open the project before running
`pip install -e .[dev]` without the diagnostics panel filling
with unactionable errors against test-only dependencies.
